commands.review_summary:
The PR extends shared plain-text tool-call repair to parse `<invoke name="...">` blocks with named parameters, buffer streamed invoke-shaped text, promote `toolUse` terminal messages, and preserve length fallback tests.

PR surface: Source +87, Tests +105. Total +192 across 8 files.

Reproducibility: yes. from source: current main does not parse invoke-style XML openings or named parameter tags in the repair package, and the embedded runner only promotes standalone text calls for `stop`. I did not run tests because this was a read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] Manual review is the right lane because no narrow automated repair remains, but the fallback parser and terminal-reason compatibility contract should be checked before merge.

target.title: Repair invoke-style plain-text tool calls
target.body:
## Summary
- Extend plain-text tool-call repair to recognize XML-ish invoke calls with named parameter arguments.
- Keep streamed invoke-style tool-call text buffered so it can be promoted into structured tool-call events instead of leaking as assistant text.
- Allow standalone text tool-call promotion when the terminal reason is `toolUse`, improving compatibility with models such as Opus 4.8 that may emit XML-ish tool-call text.
- Preserve the existing `length` fallback promotion behavior for complete under-cap plain-text tool calls.

## Behavior
This only affects malformed/plain-text tool-call repair paths. Normal structured tool calls continue to use the existing provider-native flow unchanged.

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior or issue addressed: Opus 4.8-style XML-ish plain-text tool-call output can be repaired into a structured tool-call event instead of being emitted as assistant text. The existing `length` fallback path is still promoted.
- Real environment tested: Redacted local OpenClaw checkout running this PR branch with the OpenClaw provider stream compatibility wrapper through Node/tsx.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: Ran a local OpenClaw wrapper smoke command that feeds two real wrapper inputs: an existing bracketed plain-text tool call ending with `length`, and an XML-ish invoke plain-text tool call ending with `toolUse`.
- Evidence after fix (screenshot, recording, terminal capture, console output, redacted runtime log, linked artifact, or copied live output):

```text
length fallback: event types = ["toolcall_start", "toolcall_delta", "done"]
length fallback: done.reason = "toolUse"
length fallback: tool name = "exec"
length fallback: arguments = { "command": "date" }

invoke toolUse: event types = ["toolcall_start", "toolcall_delta", "done"]
invoke toolUse: done.reason = "toolUse"
invoke toolUse: tool name = "exec"
invoke toolUse: arguments = { "command": "date" }
```

- Observed result after fix: Both compatibility paths produced structured `toolCall` content and the wrapper emitted `toolcall_start`, `toolcall_delta`, and final `done` events. The existing `length` fallback still promotes instead of passing through as assistant text.
- What was not tested: No live external model request is included in this proof; provider credentials and endpoints are intentionally omitted. Normal structured tool calls are unchanged by this path and were not re-proven here.
- Proof limitations or environment constraints: Runtime output is redacted and uses a local OpenClaw wrapper smoke run so no private endpoint, account, gateway, session, host, IP address, or token is exposed.
- Before evidence (optional but encouraged): Current main lacks support for invoke-style XML-ish openings with named parameter tags in the plain-text tool-call parser, so the same shape can leak as assistant text instead of being promoted.

## Tests and validation
- `git diff --check upstream/main`
- `corepack pnpm exec oxfmt --check packages/tool-call-repair/src/grammar.ts packages/tool-call-repair/src/payload.ts packages/tool-call-repair/src/promote.ts packages/tool-call-repair/src/stream-normalizer.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/attempt.tool-call-normalization.ts src/plugin-sdk/provider-stream-shared.test.ts src/shared/text/tool-call-shaped-text.test.ts src/shared/text/tool-call-shaped-text.ts`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.unit-fast.config.ts src/shared/text/tool-call-shaped-text.test.ts --reporter=dot --maxWorkers=1`
- Local OpenClaw wrapper smoke run covering preserved `length` fallback promotion and invoke-style `toolUse` promotion.
target.author: ralphhan
